Title: Light regulation of the biosynthesis of phenolics, terpenoids, and alkaloids in plants

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Light induces miR858a expression in a HY5-dependent manner. 2. Overexpression of miR858a rescues anthocyanin accumulation in hy5 background. 3. miR858a increases expression of anthocyanin-related genes including DFR, ANS, TT8, and GL3.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; mutant complementation; promoter binding evidence for HY5; gene expression analysis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant pigmentation and development; beneficial to human health
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts post-transcriptionally and together with HY5 regulates MYBL2 by translational inhibition and chromatin modification.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes anthocyanin accumulation while reducing lignin accumulation under light
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Light-induced miRNA7125 mediates inverse correlation between anthocyanin and lignin accumulation. 2. CCR is a target of miRNA7125. 3. MdMYB1 and MdMYB16 bind the miRNA7125 promoter to regulate its expression.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; miRNA-seq; target analysis; promoter binding assays; expression analysis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant pigmentation and health-related flavonoid function
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coordinates anthocyanin-lignin homeostasis in response to light.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes anthocyanin accumulation by repressing PySPL9
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Light induces PymiR156a expression while reducing PyPIF5 expression. 2. PymiR156a targets PySPL9, relieving repression on MBW function. 3. Overexpression of miR156 induces anthocyanin accumulation in pear fruit skin.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; promoter binding by PyPIF5; target relationship analysis; expression analysis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant pigmentation and health-related flavonoid function

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnRK1 represses anthocyanin biosynthesis at transcriptional and post-translational levels. 2. It inhibits PAP1 expression. 3. It triggers MBW complex dissociation and PAP1 degradation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional analysis summarized from cited study
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant pigmentation and development; beneficial to human health
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Negative regulator of the MBW complex in the light-response network.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: generally represses light-induced accumulation of specialized metabolites by destabilizing positive regulators
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COP1 is a core repressor of light signaling that promotes degradation of HY5 and other positive regulators in darkness. 2. COP1 negatively affects anthocyanin, carotenoid, artemisinin, and related metabolite accumulation in several species. 3. In Arabidopsis and apple, COP1 targets PAP1/PAP2-like MYBs or HY5-like regulators; in tomato silencing COP1 increases carotenoids.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; silencing; protein-protein interaction; degradation assays
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HY5; PAP1; PAP2; SPA1; MdMYB1; AaHY5; AaMYB108; AaSEP1
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: diverse defense, pigmentation, photosynthetic, and medicinal functions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as Common Gene because it is a general post-translational regulator rather than pathway enzyme or classical TF output gene in this review context.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: light signaling repressor; E3 ligase-associated factor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: represses light-mediated specialized metabolite accumulation, including carotenoids
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DET1 promotes HY5 destabilization and represses photomorphogenic metabolism in darkness. 2. Silencing DET1 enhances carotenoid accumulation in tomato. 3. DET1 also regulates BBX proteins and affects anthocyanin-related light signaling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: silencing; protein interaction evidence; degradation studies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Broad light-signaling repressor, not a direct metabolic enzyme.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: COP1-associated light signaling factor; E3 ligase complex component
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: represses anthocyanin accumulation in darkness via degradation of positive regulators
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPA works with COP1 to promote degradation of HY5 in darkness. 2. PAP1 and PAP2 interact with COP1 and SPA1 and are degraded in the dark. 3. Thus SPA contributes to dark suppression of anthocyanin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein-protein interaction; degradation assays; mutant evidence summarized

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses one coumaroyl-CoA with three malonyl-CoA molecules to produce naringenin chalcone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms early committed precursor for anthocyanin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; plant growth and reproduction; human health benefits
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum; Prunus persica; others
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS is a core light-responsive anthocyanin pathway gene in multiple species. 2. HY5 directly activates CHS in tomato and generally activates anthocyanin pathway genes. 3. CHS is also activated in pear skin by PIF8 and in peach by PpHY5-regulated light responses.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter binding; overexpression and mutant studies in cited papers; gene expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: light-induced in pigmented tissues such as fruit skin and seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light; UV-B; blue light; red light
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for enhancing anthocyanin-rich produce, but usually better manipulated through upstream regulators due to pathway coordination.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a representative pathway gene rather than a unique focal gene of the review.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; plant growth and reproduction; human health benefits
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum; Prunus persica; others
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DFR is repeatedly highlighted as a direct or indirect light-responsive target of HY5, PIFs, and MBW regulators. 2. Its promoter activity is repressed by PIF4/PIF5 under red light in Arabidopsis. 3. DFR induction is associated with increased anthocyanin accumulation across species.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter assays; TF binding; gene expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful late-pathway engineering node for anthocyanin enhancement, often together with upstream TFs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A recurrent target in multiple light-regulatory modules.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms anthocyanidin precursor of anthocyanin pigments
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; plant growth and reproduction; human health benefits
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Fragaria x ananassa; others
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANS/LDOX is a core light-responsive anthocyanin gene activated by HY5 and MBW-associated regulators. 2. MYBD or PAP1 overexpression induces ANS expression in Arabidopsis. 3. Light-associated regulators in multiple species enhance ANS transcript accumulation with higher anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene expression analyses; overexpression studies; promoter regulation evidence summarized
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Late-pathway target for stronger pigment formation when precursor supply is sufficient.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Terminal glycosylation step genes are also relevant but not emphasized here as core genes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses two molecules of geranylgeranyl pyrophosphate to form phytoene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms first committed carotenoid intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic pigment; photoprotection; coloration; nutritional value
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum; Vitis vinifera
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum; Vitis vinifera
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes carotenoid accumulation; repressed by PIFs and activated by HY5/light-associated factors
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSY is identified as the major light-regulated rate-limiting enzyme of carotenoid biosynthesis. 2. PIFs directly repress PSY in Arabidopsis and tomato. 3. HY5 antagonizes PIFs and positively regulates PSY and broader carotenoid gene sets.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter binding; mutant/overexpression analyses; gene silencing; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: light-induced; associated with chloroplast development and fruit ripening
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target for biofortification and carotenoid enhancement; stack with downstream cyclases/hydroxylases and manage photomorphogenic regulators to avoid pleiotropy.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central point of antagonistic HY5-PIF control.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes amorpha-4,11-diene through successive hydroxylation/oxidation steps toward artemisinic intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antimalarial therapeutic compound; plant defense-related metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Light upregulates CYP71AV1 expression in Artemisia annua. 2. AaGSW1 and AaWRKY14 directly activate CYP71AV1. 3. AaGSW1 together with AaMYB108 promotes CYP71AV1 expression under light regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TF-target assays; expression analysis; light treatment studies
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for raising artemisinin flux, ideally stacked with ADS, DBR2 and trichome-development regulators.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the principal light-responsive pathway genes in artemisinin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces the Delta11(13) double bond of artemisinic aldehyde to form dihydroartemisinic aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antimalarial therapeutic compound; plant defense-related metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaDBR2 is highlighted as a key light-upregulated artemisinin pathway gene. 2. AaWRKY9 directly activates AaDBR2. 3. Increased AaDBR2 expression supports artemisinin accumulation under light.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TF-target assays; expression analysis under light
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for artemisinin metabolic engineering because it directs flux toward productive intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Often considered an important branch-controlling step in Artemisia.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses one p-coumaroyl-CoA and three malonyl-CoA units to synthesize trans-resveratrol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STS15/21 are induced by UV-B and UV-C irradiation in grape. 2. Their induction correlates with increased resveratrol accumulation. 3. UV-responsive MYB14 and WRKY8 activate, whereas MYB30 represses, this branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: UV treatment expression analysis; TF regulatory studies summarized
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for enhancing resveratrol content in grape and heterologous platforms; likely best combined with UV-responsive regulators.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review specifically names STS15/21 in grape.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates tabersonine at C16 in the vindoline pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor of anticancer alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T16H2 is one of five vindoline biosynthetic genes induced by light. 2. CrGATA1 activates T16H2 expression. 3. CrPIF1 represses T16H2 expression in the dark.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene expression studies; TF-target regulation summarized
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for boosting vindoline and downstream bisindole alkaloids, especially with coordinated regulator engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the best-defined light-responsive vindoline pathway genes.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor of anticancer alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DAT is a key light-induced vindoline biosynthetic gene. 2. CrPIF1 represses DAT, whereas CrGATA1 activates DAT. 3. Because DAT catalyzes the terminal vindoline step, its induction is strongly linked to vindoline accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene expression studies; TF-target regulation summarized
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value terminal-step target for enhancing vindoline content and downstream vinblastine/vincristine production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Terminal vindoline enzyme prominently discussed in the light regulatory module.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: GAME enzymes catalyze late reactions of steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis from cholesterol-derived intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: light-induced expression promotes steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SGA biosynthesis is induced by light in tomato. 2. SlHY5 directly activates GAME1, GAME4, and GAME17. 3. SlPIF3 acts as a repressor of this light-induced module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: direct activation evidence summarized; light treatment expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: light-induced, especially in immature fruit contexts
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning defense-quality balance; caution because SGAs are toxic in edible tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated SGAs may increase toxicity and reduce food safety.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific catalytic annotation not detailed in the review; retained because directly named as HY5 target.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts methionine-derived amino acid precursors to aldoximes in aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms core precursor pool for aliphatic glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: light upregulates CYP79F1 and promotes aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79F1 is one of the glucosinolate biosynthetic genes induced by light. 2. HY5 positively regulates CYP79F1. 3. The response illustrates differential light control over aliphatic versus indolic glucosinolate branches.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: light treatment expression analysis; mutant comparison summarized
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/target for modulating aliphatic glucosinolate content in Brassicas with attention to flavor-defense tradeoffs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter pungency and antinutritional properties alongside defense benefits.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Hierarchy between HY5 and aliphatic-MYB regulators remains unresolved in the review.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS; DFR; LDOX/ANS; PAP1/MYB75; MYBD; PSY; Z-ISO; CrtISO1; LCYB; LCYE; VDE; GAME1; GAME4; GAME17; CYP79F1; CYP79B2; SOT18; Ruby; MdMYB10; MdMYBDL1; PpMYB10.1; PyMYB10; PyWD40
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates multiple pathway and regulatory genes for anthocyanin, carotenoid, artemisinin-associated, SGA, and indolic glucosinolate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of anthocyanins, carotenoids, SGAs, glucosinolates, and artemisinin-associated networks
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master light-responsive activator of specialized metabolite accumulation in many species
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: anthocyanin; carotenoid; steroidal glycoalkaloid; glucosinolate; artemisinin
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Arabidopsis; Apple; Blood orange; Peach; Pear; Tomato; Artemisia
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Malus domestica; Citrus x sinensis; Prunus persica; Pyrus pyrifolia; Solanum lycopersicum; Artemisia annua
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: same as applied in each cited case
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: generally promotes light-induced accumulation of specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HY5 is a central positive regulator downstream of photoreceptors across multiple metabolite pathways. 2. It directly activates structural and regulatory genes for anthocyanin biosynthesis in several species. 3. It positively regulates carotenoid, SGA, glucosinolate, and artemisinin-associated transcriptional networks under light.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutants; overexpression; promoter binding; transactivation; light treatment assays
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phytochromes; cryptochromes; UVR8; light-mediated COP1/SPA inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: antagonistic interplay with PIFs; part of feedforward activation of MYB regulators
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts functionally with BBX proteins and antagonistically with PIFs
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PIF1; PIF3; BBX proteins; COP1; SPA; MPK6
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light; UV-B; blue light; red light; fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad pleiotropic effects on photomorphogenesis and growth if constitutively manipulated.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Powerful master regulator for metabolite enhancement, but use tissue-specific or inducible deployment to minimize pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In glucosinolates, HY5 appears to differentially activate indolic branch but repress aliphatic-MYB regulators.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression or activation depending on member/context
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PSY; PAP1; DFR; CHS; CrGATA1; T16H2; DAT; GAME genes; PymiR156a
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: mostly represses specialized metabolite pathway genes under darkness or specific light regimes, with some positive roles such as PIF3 in far-red anthocyanin and PIF8 in pear anthocyanin
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: branch selection and transcriptional control of anthocyanin, carotenoid, vindoline, and SGA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major light-dependent repressors that relieve metabolic accumulation when degraded by light
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum; Malus domestica; Pyrus pyrifolia/Pyrus spp.; Catharanthus roseus
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: same as applied in each cited case
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: generally suppresses light-induced metabolite accumulation, though specific members can promote anthocyanin in selected contexts
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIF family members are key downstream repressors of light signaling for multiple specialized metabolites. 2. PIF1 represses PSY and carotenoid biosynthesis; PIF4/5 repress anthocyanin genes; CrPIF1 represses vindoline genes; SlPIF3 represses SGA genes. 3. Some exceptions exist, including PIF3 and PIF8 promoting anthocyanin in specific species/light conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; mutants; promoter binding; protein interaction; light treatment analyses
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: can interfere with MBW complex formation, e.g. PIF4 interaction with PAP1
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HY5; PAP1; TT8; phytochromes; cryptochromes; MdBBX23; MdERF38; MdbZIP44
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: darkness; far-red light; red light; white light; salt; BA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may strongly affect photomorphogenesis and growth architecture.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss-of-function or weakened alleles can enhance nutritional pigments/metabolites, but developmental side effects require careful tuning.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Family-level entry used because this is a review summarizing multiple PIF homologs.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation or repression via HY5-associated light signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HY5; MYB10; anthocyanin pathway genes; PSY; CCS; MYB34; MYB51
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: modulates HY5 activity or directly regulates pathway/regulatory genes, with positive or negative effects depending on member
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important modulators that fine-tune HY5-centered light responses
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Arabidopsis; Apple; Pear; Strawberry; Tomato; Pepper
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Malus domestica; Pyrus pyrifolia; Fragaria x ananassa; Solanum lycopersicum; Capsicum annuum
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: same as applied in each cited case
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: can enhance or repress light-induced metabolite accumulation depending on BBX member
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple BBX proteins positively regulate anthocyanin accumulation by enhancing HY5 or MYB expression, while others such as BBX24/25 or MdBBX37 act negatively. 2. BBX proteins also regulate carotenoid genes such as PSY or CCS and glucosinolate-associated MYBs. 3. Their functions are species- and member-specific, often mediated by protein-protein interactions with HY5 or MYBs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression/silencing summarized; protein interaction; promoter binding; expression studies
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts with HY5 and in some cases MYB proteins
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HY5; MdMYB1; MdMYB9; MdPIF7; PpHY5; FaHY5
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Member-specific pleiotropy in light development likely.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising fine-tuning regulators for fruit coloration or carotenoid traits with potentially less pleiotropy than HY5 if member-specific genes are used.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Family-level entry used because the review synthesizes multiple BBX homologs across species.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR; ANS and other anthocyanin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes as part of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: core MBW activator for anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAP1 is directly induced by HY5 in Arabidopsis and functions in the MBW complex. 2. Apple MdMYB1/10 are PAP1-like MYBs activated by MdHY5 and central for anthocyanin accumulation. 3. PAP1 protein stability is negatively controlled by COP1/SPA and positively influenced by MAPK4 or SUMOylation as discussed in the review.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; promoter binding; protein degradation assays; protein interaction studies
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with bHLH and WD40 proteins
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TT8; GL3; EGL3; TTG1; COP1; SPA1; PIF4
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can divert phenylpropanoid flux from other branches.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for fruit and crop anthocyanin enhancement; combine with stabilization strategies and tissue-specific expression.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Grouped PAP1 and PAP1-like orthologs because review emphasizes conserved role.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression of anthocyanin repressors / activation of pathway indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis by repressing MYBL2 and inducing pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive intermediate regulator downstream of HY5
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HY5 binds the MYBD promoter and induces its expression in Arabidopsis. 2. MYBD overexpression induces DFR and ANS expression. 3. MYBD represses MYBL2 and in apple MdMYBDL1 acts similarly in light-induced anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter binding; overexpression studies; gene expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful supportive regulator to strengthen anthocyanin output while relieving repression.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYBD overexpression affects MYBL2 promoter acetylation status, linking light control with epigenetic regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates five light-responsive vindoline biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key positive regulator mediating light induction of vindoline pathway
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrGATA1 acts as a positive regulator of vindoline biosynthesis. 2. It activates five light-responsive vindoline genes. 3. Its expression is repressed by CrPIF1 in darkness and released under light.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional analysis and target expression studies summarized
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: light; CrPIF1 repression relieved by light
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for engineering vindoline and downstream anticancer alkaloids with potentially better specificity than general light signaling components.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Proposed as central switch linking light signaling to vindoline branch genes.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses four key camptothecin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: mediates light-induced suppression of camptothecin production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Light induces CaLMF expression. 2. CaLMF represses CaTDC1, CaG8O, CaCYC1, and Ca7-DLS. 3. This repression underlies light-mediated suppression of camptothecin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization summarized; target gene expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced camptothecin may weaken defense or medicinal yield.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate knockout/repression target to enhance camptothecin production in cultivation or biofactory systems.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although sequence-similar to AtHY5, the review notes HY5-like function needs further validation.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS; DFR; PSY; Z-ISO; CrtISO1; LCYB; LCYE; VDE; GAME1; GAME4; GAME17
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates anthocyanin, carotenoid, and SGA biosynthetic genes in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin, carotenoid, and steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive light-responsive regulator in tomato fruit metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes blue-light-induced anthocyanin, carotenoid accumulation, and SGA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlHY5 directly activates CHS and DFR for blue-light-induced anthocyanin accumulation. 2. It directly regulates multiple carotenoid genes, showing broader control than PIFs. 3. It directly activates GAME1, GAME4, and GAME17 in light-induced SGA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: direct promoter binding; silencing/functional studies summarized; light treatment assays
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: light; COP1/DET1 repression relieved by light
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: blue light; fruit ripening; general light exposure
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could increase toxic SGAs if broadly activated in fruit; may affect development.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for carotenoid-rich and pigmented tomato improvement, but edible-tissue SGA effects require careful balancing.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tomato-specific HY5 functions are especially broad in this review.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates WRKY regulators that induce artemisinin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: central positive light regulator of artemisinin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaHY5 positively regulates artemisinin biosynthesis. 2. It directly induces three WRKY TFs, AaGSW1, AaWRKY9, and AaWRKY14. 3. Both AaHY5 and AaMYB108 are targeted by AaCOP1, integrating light-dependent protein stability with artemisinin production.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization and target activation studies summarized
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light; jasmonate discussed as interacting cue
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad light-signaling functions may complicate whole-plant phenotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for boosting artemisinin via trichome-targeted or inducible expression combined with pathway-gene stacking.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of multiple artemisinin-specific regulators rather than directly on all enzyme genes.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates CYP71AV1 and indirectly induces other pathway enzymes through AaORA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important trichome-associated positive regulator connecting light signaling to pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaGSW1 is directly induced downstream of AaHY5. 2. It directly activates CYP71AV1 and activates AaORA to induce additional pathway genes. 3. It interacts with AaMYB108 to induce CYP71AV1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: target activation assays; interaction studies; functional characterization summarized
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value positive regulator for trichome-focused metabolic engineering of artemisinin.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also connected with trichome development-associated regulation in Artemisia.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses vindoline regulatory and pathway genes in darkness
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key dark repressor released by light-dependent degradation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrPIF1 negatively regulates vindoline biosynthesis. 2. It represses CrGATA1 as well as T16H2 and DAT. 3. Light-triggered degradation of CrPIF1 releases CrGATA1-dependent induction of vindoline genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization and target analysis summarized
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulation or editing may enhance vindoline accumulation, especially in controlled light production systems.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A clean example of conserved PIF-mediated repression in alkaloid metabolism.
